text.title: design-critique Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-critique as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Without stated goals and design principles, critique sessions default to preference debates regardless of format.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without explicit goals, every comment is opinion. Goals are the foundation of structured critique.

failure-mode.description: Power dynamics suppress critique quality: junior designers do not give honest feedback to senior designers in hierarchical teams without explicit facilitation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Facilitation must create psychological safety. Ground rules posted in the room, explicit permission for juniors to speak, and framing feedback as "about the work" all help.

failure-mode.description: Async critique loses the clarifying question phase; written comments often assume rather than ask.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Async critique requires higher structure (format + examples + rubric) to compensate for missing real-time clarification. Build this in from the start.

failure-mode.description: "Design by committee" failure mode: when everyone in the room redesigns rather than analyzes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Redirect solutioning back to analysis. Facilitator frames: "That's a great idea. Does the current design fail against a goal, or are you proposing an alternative?"
